seo.title: Osaji no Yado Doyashiki — A 300-Year-Old Farmhouse, All to Yourself.
seo.description: Nishiawakura, Okayama. One group per night in a historic kominka. River play, open-fire cooking, irori hearth, stargazing. Slow down and reconnect.

hero.label: Osaji no Yado · Doyashiki
hero.heading:
Live, Play,
and Be.
hero.sub:
A 300-year-old farmhouse in a mountain village — just for you.
Swim in the river. Gather around the fire. Sleep under the stars.

body: Welcome to Osaji no Yado Doyashiki, nestled in Nishiawakura village, Okayama. We offer exclusive use of a 300-year-old farmhouse — one group per night. No elaborate service, no kaiseki cuisine. Just the sound of the river, the warmth of the irori hearth, and a sky full of stars.
One guest called it "a place that feels like Japan's original landscape."

experiences.title: Play in the River
experiences.body: A clear mountain stream runs right in front of the house. Shriek at the cold water. Let the kids run wild.
experiences.quote: "The kids were so excited about river swimming. We could splash around right in front of the house."

experiences.title: Rest on the Engawa
experiences.body: Cook rice in a hagama pot, slice watermelon on the veranda. Gaze at the mountains and do absolutely nothing. That's the treat.
experiences.quote: "The kids got to cook rice in the hagama. The adults enjoyed drinks surrounded by nature."

experiences.title: Gather Around the Fire
experiences.body: At night, we light the irori hearth. Talk by firelight alone. You'll find yourself a little more honest than usual.
experiences.quote: "Around the irori fire, even my usually-quiet mother wouldn't stop talking."

experiences.title: Feel the Forest
experiences.body: Nishiawakura sits deep in the Chugoku mountains. Venture to the Wakasugi Natural Forest for a walk among ancient trees.
experiences.quote: "There's an old-growth forest just 10 minutes by car — the forest bathing was wonderful."

reservation.heading: This Summer, With Family.
reservation.sub: Fireflies and starlit skies are waiting.

faq.a: We offer accommodation only. The kitchen is yours to use freely. We can help arrange grocery runs.
Food, your favourite drinks, and insect repellent if bugs bother you.

faq.q: Is it okay to bring young children?
faq.a: Absolutely. The whole house is yours, so children can be themselves. Adult supervision required for river play.

faq.q: Can I get there by train?
faq.a: About 10 minutes' walk from Awakura Onsen Station. We can discuss pick-up for grocery trips.
Yes — it's an old farmhouse. Stink bugs are common (the village calls them 'Kame-chan'). We'll show you the local trick for handling them.
Summer nights are cool. Heating is provided in winter.

access.heading: Getting Here
access.car: About 5 minutes from Nishiawakura IC (Tottori Expressway). Free parking. Easy to reach despite the mountain setting.
access.train: About 10 minutes' walk from Awakura Onsen Station (Chizu Express). Pick-up available on request.
